benchmark_set: NTHMP required tsunami inundation benchmarks for Celeris-WebGPU

authoritative_sources.title: Summary of NTHMP Tsunami Inundation Benchmark Problems
authoritative_sources.role: Required case list, checklists, and acceptance targets

authoritative_sources.title: NTHMP Mapping and Modeling Subcommittee benchmarking page
authoritative_sources.role: Criteria, process, benchmark problem, and workshop links

authoritative_sources.title: NOAA Center for Tsunami Research benchmark pages
authoritative_sources.role: PMEL-135 descriptions and direct benchmark data links

authoritative_sources.title: 2011 NTHMP workshop benchmark repository
authoritative_sources.role: Workshop descriptions, reference data, observations, and figures

inputs: optional etaInitCond.txt for gridded initial free-surface fields
optional side-specific boundary time-series text files
execution: Run in a WebGPU-enabled Chromium browser
Use Selenium automation where repeatable browser execution is needed
Prefer per-benchmark runner scripts run_bp01.py, run_bp04.py, run_bp06.py, run_bp07.py, and run_bp09.py for generated benchmark cases
outputs: max free-surface or wave-height surfaces when triggered
benchmark-local metrics JSON/CSV and figures generated from browser downloads

benchmarks.title: Single wave on simple beach
acceptance_targets: Maximum runup of the nonbreaking solitary wave within 5% of analytical solution
Numerical experiments over a range of depths compared to each other and the analytical solution
checklist: Compute maximum runup
Compute eta/d at t = 25, 35, 45, 55, and 65 sqrt(d/g)
Compute eta/d time series at x/d = 0.25 and x/d = 9.95
Demonstrate scalability
celeris_setup_plan: Generate a canonical sloping-beach bathymetry with a narrow 2D flume width
Run NLSW first, with reflective sidewalls and the benchmark shoreline geometry
Represent the benchmark initial wave with Celeris native Add Disturbance by setting add_Disturbance = 1 and disturbanceType = 1, so eta and momentum are applied before the first timestep
Sample model eta at x/d = 0.25 and x/d = 9.95
Extract maximum runup from max free-surface over initially dry beach cells
outputs_to_generate: Scalability table for at least two grid resolutions

benchmarks.title: Single wave on simple beach
summary_cases: Case B optional per workshop summary
acceptance_targets: Nonbreaking maximum runup within 5% of laboratory measurements
Breaking-wave predictions within 10% of laboratory measurements
Consistent prediction of R/d versus H/d variation
checklist: Compute eta/d profiles at t = 25, 35, 45, 55, and 65 sqrt(d/g) for Case A
Compute eta/d profiles at t = 10, 15, 20, 25, and 30 sqrt(d/g) for Case C
Compute maximum runup for Case A and Case C
Compute maximum runup R/d versus H/d

reference_files.role: PMEL Case A experimental profile data
PMEL Case C experimental profile data

celeris_setup_plan: Generate the 1:19.85 canonical beach and constant-depth flume region
Use NLSW mode first; run breaking-model sensitivity for Case C
Use Celeris native Add Disturbance solitary-wave initialization for Case A and Case C rather than an initial free-surface file
Generate Case A with d = 0.30 m and Case C with d = 0.15 m, matching the approximate depths stated in the benchmark description
Sample centerline eta profiles at requested nondimensional times
Extract maximum runup using one documented wet/dry threshold
outputs_to_generate: Case A and Case C profile overlays
Maximum runup table with percent error

benchmarks.title: Solitary wave on a conical island
summary_cases: Case A optional per workshop summary
acceptance_targets: Stable splitting of wave fronts in front of island and collision behind it
Back-island collision runup within 20% of laboratory measurements
checklist: Show wave-front splitting and back-island collision
Compute eta/d time series at gauges 9, 16, and 22
Compute runup R/d around the island
celeris_setup_plan: Generate analytic conical-island bathymetry in a 30 m by 25 m laboratory basin
Use d = 0.32 m water depth and 1:4 island slope geometry
Use Celeris native Add Disturbance solitary-wave initialization from the generator side for Cases B and C
Use the official basin coordinate system with island center at (12.96, 13.80) m and toe radius 3.55 m inferred from the gauge-depth table
Place output gauges at the official gauge coordinates
Extract runup around the island perimeter by angular station
outputs_to_generate: Gauge 9, 16, and 22 overlays for Cases B and C
Runup R/d polar plot around the island
Snapshot figure showing split and collision

benchmarks.title: Tsunami runup onto a complex three-dimensional model of Monai Valley beach
acceptance_targets: Rapid withdrawal and runup sequence represented
Maximum runup in the narrow valley within 10% of laboratory/reference results
checklist: Compute snapshots corresponding to movie frames 10, 25, 40, 55, and 70 at 0.5 s frame spacing
Compute eta at gauges 1, 2, and 3
Compute maximum runup in the narrow valley
celeris_setup_plan: Convert official bathymetry to Celeris bathy.txt preserving 1.4 cm recommended spacing where feasible
Use reflective sidewalls at y = 0 and y = 3.5 m
Convert measured incident wave to a west boundary type-5 eta/hu/hv time-series forcing file
Extract maximum runup in the Monai valley zone
outputs_to_generate: Maximum runup metric and inundation/runup map

benchmarks.title: Okushiri Island tsunami
acceptance_targets: Maximum runup at Aonae within 20% of measurements
First arrival at Aonae about 5 minutes after earthquake
Two waves at Aonae about 10 minutes apart with west/east arrival behavior
Tide-gauge and runup distributions reproduce key observations
checklist: Compute runup around Aonae
Compute arrival of first wave to Aonae
Show two waves at Aonae approximately 10 minutes apart
Compute water level at Iwanai and Esashi tide gauges
Compute maximum modeled runup distribution around Okushiri Island
Compute modeled runup at Hamatsumae
Compute modeled runup at the valley north of Monai

reference_files.role: PMEL Region A bathymetry/topography, 379 by 687 at 450 m spacing
PMEL Region A DCRC-17a initial free-surface, 379 by 687 at 450 m spacing
PMEL Region B1 south Okushiri bathymetry/topography, 154 by 124 at 150 m spacing
PMEL Region B1 DCRC-17a initial free-surface, 154 by 124 at 150 m spacing
PMEL Region C1 Aonae bathymetry/topography, 112 by 94 at 50 m spacing
PMEL Region C1 Aonae DCRC-17a initial free-surface, 112 by 94 at 50 m spacing
PMEL Region C23 Monai bathymetry/topography, 40 by 202 at 50 m spacing
PMEL Region C23 Monai DCRC-17a initial free-surface, 40 by 202 at 50 m spacing

celeris_setup_plan: Follow the Pearl Harbor master_nested_config.m pattern: define a master table of grid bounds, per-child refinement factors, grid_type = 2 spherical NLSW, and parent-child nesting relationships
Use a default A-grid base refinement of 4x relative to PMEL Region A spacing and a default 4x child-grid refinement at each nesting level
Run one-way nested grids: Grid A parent, Grid B south Okushiri child, branched Grid C/D Aonae child nests, and Grid C/D/E Monai child nests
Interpolate HNO1993 ocean-bottom deformation to etaInitCond.txt on Grid A only; child grids start from still water and are driven by parent-generated type-5 boundary files
Use source-priority bathymetry interpolation: PMEL A/B/C fallbacks plus OK03 around Okushiri, AO15 around Aonae, MO01 around Monai, and MB05 for the final Monai grid
Use Celeris nestedGridOutput_rectangles on parent grids to write boundary type 5 eta/hu/hv files for each child grid with child-specific file prefixes
Place tide gauges at Iwanai and Esashi plus Aonae and Monai diagnostics
Extract runup distribution from maximum free surface and compare with FieldData.xlsx
outputs_to_generate: Aonae arrival and two-wave diagnostic plot
